more.title: How the Skeleton of Mutapa Times is Based on Sarah Charlesworth's Work
more.description: Conceptual artist Sarah Charlesworth's 1979 study of how newspapers covered a solar eclipse inspired the framework behind The Mutapa Times.

more.title: Woman strangles to death newly-born baby, buries body in shallow grave
more.description: IN an act of sheer cruelty, a 32-year-old woman from Mvurwi in Mashonaland Central delivered a bouncing baby girl, strangled her to death before shoving the body in a shallow grave. Police confirmed the incident which led to the arrest of Thandiwe Kangara last week. “The ZRP confirms the arrest of Thandiwe Kangara (32) in [...]The post Woman strangles to death newly-born baby, buries body in shallow grave appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com.

more.title: Defiant Madhuku vows to stop Mnangagwa’s term extension despite crackdown on anti-2030 proponents
more.description: LOVEMORE Madhuku, leader of opposition National Constitutional Assembly (NCA) party, has vowed to mobilise citizens to stop proposals to rewrite the country’s Constitution inorder to extend President Emmerson Mnangagwa’s term of office, among other governance changes. A defiant Madhuku, who was recently beaten up by unknown assailants alongside his supporters for resisting Constitutional Amendment Number [...]The post Defiant Madhuku vows to stop Mnangagwa’s term extension despite crackdown on anti-2030 proponents appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com.

more.title: In post-colonial Africa, the ethical neutrality of AI is pure fantasy
more.description: If we treat AI as a purely rational evolution of human intelligence, we risk repeating colonial erasure on a digital scale, says Agnieszka Piotrowska “Knowledge production, including the production of technology, is one of the most important political questions facing higher education globally.” When Diana Jeater, professor of African history at the University of Liverpool, [...]The post In post-colonial Africa, the ethical neutrality of AI is pure fantasy appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.
